A.5.1.proof: ISMS policy suite approved by executive management and published on intranet. Annual review completed Feb 2026. Statement of Applicability (SoA) version 4.2 current.
A.5.1.notes: Policy refresh aligned with ISO 27001:2022 transition.

A.5.2.proof: RACI matrix for all ISMS roles maintained in GRC platform. Information security responsibilities included in job descriptions and performance objectives.

A.5.3.proof: Segregation of duties policy documented. Critical process workflows reviewed for SoD conflicts. Access control matrix enforces separation for financial and IT admin functions.
A.5.3.plan: Implement automated SoD conflict detection in IAM platform.

A.5.4.proof: Management commitment demonstrated through ISMS steering committee meetings (monthly). Budget allocation for information security approved annually. Internal audit findings tracked to closure.

A.5.5.proof: Contact register maintained for relevant authorities (data protection regulators, law enforcement, sector CERTs). Escalation procedures documented in incident response plan.

A.5.6.proof: Membership in industry security forums and ISACs. Informal engagement with peer organizations on threat intelligence sharing.
A.5.6.plan: Formalize participation in two additional special interest groups relevant to our sector.

A.5.7.proof: Threat intelligence feeds integrated into SIEM. Weekly threat briefings produced by security operations team. Quarterly threat landscape reports presented to ISMS steering committee.

A.5.8.proof: Project security gate reviews mandatory per SDLC policy. Security requirements captured in project initiation documents. Risk assessments performed at design phase for all projects.
A.5.8.notes: Verified during last internal audit cycle.

A.5.9.proof: Asset inventory maintained in CMDB with automated discovery. Hardware, software, cloud services, and data assets cataloged. Quarterly reconciliation against procurement records.

A.5.10.proof: Acceptable use policy covers information assets, IT equipment, email, internet, and BYOD. Signed by all employees during onboarding. Annual re-acknowledgment required.

A.5.11.proof: Return of assets checklist integrated into HR offboarding workflow. IT asset recovery tracked in ticketing system. Remote wipe capability for mobile devices.

A.5.12.proof: Four-tier classification scheme (Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted) documented in ISMS. Classification guidelines published with examples for each level.

A.5.13.proof: Labelling procedures documented but inconsistently applied for electronic documents. Physical media labelling enforced. Email classification headers not yet automated.
A.5.13.plan: Deploy automated document classification and labelling tool integrated with DLP by Q3 2026.

A.5.14.proof: Information transfer policy covers electronic, physical, and verbal transfers. TLS enforced for email and file transfers. Secure file sharing platform deployed for external transfers.

A.5.15.proof: Access control policy based on role-based access model. Access provisioning integrated with HR lifecycle. Quarterly access reviews completed for all critical systems.

A.5.16.proof: Centralized identity management via Okta. Unique user IDs enforced. Service account governance process established with regular credential rotation.

A.5.17.proof: Password policy enforces complexity, length (14+ chars), and history requirements. MFA deployed for all remote access and privileged accounts. Breach password checking enabled.
A.5.17.plan: Migrate to passwordless authentication for corporate accounts by Q4 2026.

A.5.18.proof: Access rights provisioned through automated IAM workflows. Joiners-movers-leavers process integrated with HR system. Privileged access managed through PAM solution.

A.5.19.proof: Supplier security policy documented. Security requirements included in procurement process. Risk-based supplier categorization (Critical, High, Medium, Low) established.

A.5.20.proof: Standard security clauses in supplier agreements covering data protection, incident notification, right to audit, and compliance obligations. Legal review for all critical supplier contracts.

A.5.21.proof: ICT supply chain risks identified for key technology vendors. Software composition analysis for critical applications. Not yet extended to full supply chain depth.
A.5.21.plan: Implement SBOM requirements for all critical software suppliers. Extend supply chain risk assessments to sub-tier suppliers.
A.5.21.notes: Board-level priority following recent supply chain incidents in the industry.

A.5.22.proof: Annual supplier security assessments for critical vendors. SOC 2/ISO 27001 certificates collected. Change notification requirements in contracts.

A.5.23.proof: Cloud security policy documented. CASB deployed for shadow IT detection. Cloud service risk assessments performed before onboarding. CSA STAR registry checked for cloud providers.

A.5.24.proof: Incident management procedure documented with severity classification, escalation matrix, and communication templates. Tested via tabletop exercises semi-annually.

A.5.25.proof: Event triage procedure with defined criteria for escalation from event to incident. SOC analysts trained on classification. SIEM correlation rules automate initial assessment.

A.5.26.proof: Incident response playbooks for 10 common incident types. SOAR platform automates containment actions. Post-incident reviews conducted for all P1/P2 incidents.

A.5.27.proof: Post-incident review process documented. Lessons learned captured and tracked to implementation. Root cause analysis performed for all significant incidents.

A.5.28.proof: Evidence collection procedures documented. Chain of custody forms available. Forensic imaging capability exists but not all analysts trained on evidence handling.
A.5.28.plan: Provide forensic evidence handling training to all SOC analysts.

A.5.29.proof: Business continuity requirements for information security documented in BCP. Security controls maintained during disruption scenarios as tested in DR exercises.

A.5.30.proof: ICT continuity plans exist for Tier 1 systems. Recovery objectives (RTO/RPO) defined. Semi-annual DR testing for critical applications.
A.5.30.plan: Extend ICT continuity planning to Tier 2 systems. Automate failover for cloud workloads.

A.5.31.proof: Legal and regulatory requirements register maintained in GRC tool. GDPR, local data protection laws, and sector-specific regulations tracked. Compliance obligations mapped to ISMS controls.

A.5.32.proof: IPR policy documented. Software licensing compliance tracked via SAM tool. Open source usage governed by approved license list. Legal review for IP-sensitive projects.

A.5.33.proof: Records management policy with retention schedules aligned to legal requirements. Automated retention enforcement in email and document management systems.

A.5.34.proof: Privacy program aligned with GDPR and applicable local laws. Privacy impact assessments performed for new processing activities. DPO appointed and operational.
A.5.34.notes: Annual privacy audit completed with no significant findings.

A.5.35.proof: Annual independent ISMS audit conducted by accredited certification body. Internal audit program covers all Annex A controls over a 3-year cycle. Surveillance audit passed Dec 2025.

A.5.36.proof: Compliance monitoring process established. Technical compliance checked via vulnerability scans and configuration audits. Policy compliance verified through internal audits.

A.5.37.proof: Operating procedures documented for all critical IT processes. Procedures reviewed annually and updated upon significant changes. Version control maintained.

A.6.1.proof: Background checks performed for all employees before employment. Enhanced vetting for roles with access to sensitive information. Screening policy documented and consistently applied.

A.6.2.proof: Employment contracts include information security responsibilities, confidentiality obligations, and acceptable use terms. Reviewed by legal and HR annually.

A.6.3.proof: Mandatory annual security awareness training for all staff via LMS. Role-based training for developers, IT admins, and management. Monthly phishing simulations with targeted remediation.
A.6.3.notes: Training completion rate: 98%. Phishing click rate: 2.8%.

A.6.4.proof: Disciplinary process for security policy violations documented in HR policy. Graduated response framework (warning, formal action, termination). Tracked in HR system.

A.6.5.proof: Post-employment obligations defined in contracts (NDA, IP assignment). Offboarding checklist includes access revocation, asset return, and exit interview covering security obligations.

A.6.6.proof: NDA templates for employees, contractors, and third parties. Signed before access to confidential information granted. Tracked in contract management system.

A.6.7.proof: Remote working policy covers security requirements for home offices. VPN required for network access. Endpoint security controls enforced regardless of location.

A.6.8.proof: Security event reporting procedure published and accessible to all staff. Multiple reporting channels (email, phone hotline, web form). Awareness training covers reporting obligations.

A.7.1.proof: Physical security perimeters defined for all facilities. Data centers in Tier III certified facilities with layered access controls. Office buildings with reception and badge access.

A.7.2.proof: Badge-based access control system at all entry points. Biometric authentication for data center access. Visitor management system with pre-registration and escort requirements.

A.7.3.proof: Server rooms and network closets secured with electronic access controls. Clean desk policy enforced through periodic spot checks. Secure print solution deployed.

A.7.4.proof: CCTV coverage at all entry/exit points and sensitive areas. 90-day retention of footage. Intrusion detection systems in data centers. 24/7 monitoring by security operations.

A.7.5.proof: Environmental controls documented for data centers (fire suppression, flood detection, HVAC). Risk assessments for natural hazards completed for all facilities.

A.7.6.proof: Secure area procedures documented including restrictions on photography, personal devices, and unescorted visitors. Signage posted at secure area entry points.
A.7.6.notes: Primarily cloud-based infrastructure reduces need for on-premises secure areas.

A.7.7.proof: Clear desk and clear screen policy documented. Auto-lock configured on all workstations (5-minute timeout). Periodic compliance checks by facilities team.

A.7.8.proof: Equipment placement considers physical security and environmental factors. Cable management and power protection documented. Data center equipment in locked racks.

A.7.9.proof: Guidelines for off-premises equipment exist. Laptop encryption enforced. Mobile device management deployed. No formal tracking of equipment taken off-site.
A.7.9.plan: Implement equipment check-out tracking system for sensitive assets.

A.7.10.proof: Storage media handling procedures cover classification, encryption, transport, and disposal. Encrypted USB drives issued for authorized use. Removable media policy enforced via endpoint controls.

A.7.11.proof: UPS and generator backup for data center facilities. Redundant power feeds for critical systems. Regular testing of backup power systems documented.

A.7.12.proof: Structured cabling standards followed in data centers. Cable management documented. Physical separation of power and network cabling where required.

A.7.13.proof: Preventive maintenance schedule for all critical equipment. Maintenance performed by authorized personnel only. Maintenance records maintained in asset management system.

A.7.14.proof: Media sanitization procedures follow NIST 800-88 guidelines. Certificates of destruction obtained for physical media. Verified data erasure for equipment reuse.

A.8.1.proof: Endpoint security suite deployed on all managed devices (EDR, encryption, DLP). MDM for mobile devices. BYOD policy with containerization for corporate data access.

A.8.2.proof: PAM solution manages privileged access. Just-in-time privilege escalation implemented. Privileged session recording enabled. Quarterly privileged access reviews completed.

A.8.3.proof: Application-level access controls enforce need-to-know. Data access governed by classification level. API access controls with OAuth2 and token-based authentication.

A.8.4.proof: Source code repositories secured with role-based access. Branch protection rules enforced. Code review required before merge to main branches.

A.8.5.proof: MFA enforced for all authentication scenarios. SSO via SAML/OIDC for cloud applications. Adaptive authentication based on risk signals (location, device, behavior).

A.8.6.proof: Capacity monitoring dashboards for infrastructure and applications. Auto-scaling configured for cloud workloads. Capacity planning reviews performed quarterly.

A.8.7.proof: EDR with real-time malware detection on all endpoints. Email gateway with advanced threat protection. Sandboxing for suspicious attachments. Definitions updated automatically.

A.8.8.proof: Vulnerability management program with weekly scanning. CVSS-based prioritization. Critical vulnerabilities patched within 7 days SLA. Vulnerability metrics reported monthly.
A.8.8.notes: Average MTTR for critical vulnerabilities: 4.5 days.

A.8.9.proof: Configuration baselines established using CIS Benchmarks. Configuration management via infrastructure-as-code. Drift detection and automated remediation deployed.

A.8.10.proof: Data retention and deletion procedures documented. Automated retention policies in email and document systems. Secure deletion verified for decommissioned storage.

A.8.11.proof: Data masking implemented for production data used in test environments. Not yet applied consistently across all non-production environments.
A.8.11.plan: Extend data masking to all development and testing environments. Implement dynamic data masking for analytics workloads.

A.8.12.proof: DLP policies configured for email and cloud storage. Endpoint DLP deployed on managed devices. Network DLP monitoring outbound traffic for sensitive data patterns.
A.8.12.plan: Extend DLP coverage to collaboration platforms and API egress points.

A.8.13.proof: Backup strategy follows 3-2-1 rule. Daily incremental, weekly full backups. Immutable backups for critical systems. Quarterly restore tests documented and verified.

A.8.14.proof: Redundant processing facilities for critical systems. Active-passive configuration for databases. Cloud workloads deployed across multiple availability zones.

A.8.15.proof: Centralized logging via SIEM with 90-day hot storage, 1-year cold retention. Log sources include endpoints, network devices, applications, cloud platforms, and identity systems.

A.8.16.proof: 24/7 SOC monitoring via SIEM. UEBA for anomaly detection. Network traffic analysis for lateral movement. Alert triage within 15-minute SLA for critical events.

A.8.17.proof: NTP synchronization configured across all systems. Authoritative time source established. Clock drift monitoring and alerting in place.

A.8.18.proof: Privileged utility programs restricted to authorized administrators. Usage logged and monitored. Approval required for utility program installation on production systems.

A.8.19.proof: Application whitelisting enforced on servers. Software installation restricted on endpoints via group policy. Approved software catalog maintained and enforced.

A.8.20.proof: Network segmented into security zones with firewall controls. Zero trust network access for remote users. Microsegmentation for critical workloads.

A.8.21.proof: SLAs for network services include security requirements. Network service provider security assessed during procurement. Managed firewall and DDoS protection services monitored.

A.8.22.proof: Network segregation enforced between development, test, and production environments. DMZ for public-facing services. Management network isolated from user networks.

A.8.23.proof: Web filtering policy deployed via secure web gateway. Category-based blocking for malicious, phishing, and policy-violating sites. HTTPS inspection enabled.

A.8.24.proof: Cryptography policy defines approved algorithms and key lengths. TLS 1.2+ enforced for all communications. Key management procedures documented. HSMs for critical key material.

A.8.25.proof: SSDLC documented covering threat modeling, secure coding standards, code review, and security testing. Security gates at each phase of development lifecycle.

A.8.26.proof: Security requirements derived from risk assessments and classification. OWASP ASVS used as reference for web application requirements. Not fully integrated into all project types.
A.8.26.plan: Standardize security requirements templates across all application types including APIs and microservices.

A.8.27.proof: Secure architecture principles documented (defense-in-depth, least privilege, fail-safe). Reference architectures published for common deployment patterns. Architecture review board includes security.

A.8.28.proof: Secure coding guidelines published for primary languages. SAST integrated in CI/CD pipeline. Developer training on secure coding conducted annually.
A.8.28.plan: Expand SAST coverage to all repositories. Implement real-time secure coding assistance in IDEs.

A.8.29.proof: Security testing requirements defined per risk level. DAST for web applications before release. Penetration testing for critical applications. Acceptance criteria include security sign-off.

A.8.30.proof: Security requirements included in outsourced development contracts. Code review performed for outsourced deliverables. Vendor SDLC maturity not consistently assessed.
A.8.30.plan: Implement SDLC maturity assessment as part of development vendor onboarding.

A.8.31.proof: Separate environments for development, testing, staging, and production. Network segregation enforced. Production data not used in lower environments without masking.

A.8.32.proof: Change management process with CAB review for production changes. Changes tested in staging. Rollback procedures required. Emergency change process defined with post-hoc review.

A.8.33.proof: Test data management procedures documented. Production data anonymized or masked for test use. Synthetic test data generation available for development teams.

A.8.34.proof: Audit testing considerations addressed informally when external audits are planned. No formal documented procedures for protecting systems during audit testing.
A.8.34.plan: Document formal procedures for managing information system access and protections during audit activities.
